sizing_rule.note: Split-equity technique: divide total equity into inactive reserve and active equity. Calculate contract size as: Active Equity ÷ (Equity per Contract at Optimal f). Example: $55,000 active equity ÷ $2,500 per contract = 22 contracts. Optimal f level given as 1 contract per $2,500 active equity.

source_rule_text: Use split-equity technique to allocate an inactive equity reserve while calculating contract size from active equity at optimal f level
